recaps.text: In A Deadly Education, El Higgins survived part of her senior year at the Scholomance, a lethal wizard school with no teachers. Born with an affinity for mass destruction that she refuses to use, and poor and unaffiliated in a world run by powerful enclaves, she got by on sheer will. New York's celebrated Orion Lake kept saving her life until, to her surprise, they became friends, and she built a genuine alliance with the artificer Aadhya and the musician Liu. Choosing to protect the whole class rather than only the privileged, she unleashed a glimpse of her true power to clear the deadly graduation hall, and ended the year with a warning from her mother to keep away from Orion.

The senior class's final year begins with the school itself seeming to single El out, burying her under lethal workloads and funneling the most vulnerable students into her care. Newly surrounded by would-be allies who now see her as powerful rather than pathetic, El is uneasy with her own popularity. She and Orion grow closer even as she wrestles with what her destructive gift is actually for, and the ceaseless threat of maleficaria makes every single day a fight just to reach graduation.

El comes to suspect the school may be pushing her toward a purpose: protecting the students no enclave would ever bother to save. With Aadhya, Liu, Chloe, and a widening circle around her, she begins to imagine a graduation where everyone gets out, not only the rich and connected, and the sharp, competitive enclaver Liesel is drawn into the planning. But the number of monsters loose in the school keeps rising to unnatural levels, and it grows clear that something much larger is going wrong.

El's impossible ambition, to send the entire senior class safely through graduation, hardens into a concrete plan that binds independents and enclavers into one fragile alliance. The maleficaria are massing in unprecedented numbers, and the strain of holding everyone together tests every relationship, including El's deepening bond with Orion. Old assumptions about enclave power and about who deserves to be rescued are challenged as the class drills and prepares for the gauntlet waiting between them and the outside world.

The seniors execute their audacious plan and, against the odds, El's magic and her alliance carry the whole class toward the exit. But the escape turns catastrophic at the graduation hall, where the monstrous maw-mouth Patience stands between them and freedom. In the chaos El is forced out through the gates while Orion stays behind to hold the horror off, and the doors seal with him still inside. El lands back in the ordinary world alive and graduated but shattered, vowing to find a way back for him.
